title: Bio-Plex phosphoprotein assay using spinal cords and skeletal muscles of mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c3ksm","text":"Bio-Plex phosphoprotein assays are magnetic bead-based immunoassays for the detection of intracellular phosphoproteins in cell and tissue lysates. The assay can be readily multiplexed to simultaneously examine the expression levels of numerous phosphorylated proteins.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"damya","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Cut the tissue into small pieces (~3x3 mm) in a 1.5 ml tissue grinder
Add 10 times volume of cell lysis buffer and grind the tissue on ice using 20 strokes
Freeze samples at -80℃. Freezing and thawing samples helps increase cell lysis effects.
Thaw the sample and sonicate on ice (1 min)
Centrifuge at 15000xg for 10 min at 4℃
Collect the supernatant
Measure the protein concentration using DC TM Protein Assay and a plate reader
Adjust the concentration of protein to 600µg/ml with cell lysis buffer
Freeze samples at -80℃
Thaw sample lysates and keep on ice
Reconstitute lyophilized cell lysate control with 250 µl of dH 2 O, vortex for 5 sec to mix and incubate at room temperature for 20 min
Centrifuge all samples and lysate controls at 15000xg for 10 min at 4℃
Vortex the 20x coupled beads at mid speed for 30 sec and carefully open the cap
Dilute coupled beads to 1x with wash buffer, vortex, protect from light with aluminum foil. For the assay in 96wells, mix 288 µl of 20x beads with 5472 µl of wash buffer.
Vortex the 1xbeads for 15 sec at medium speed and transfer 50 µl to each well of the assay plate
Wash the plate two times with 200 µl wash buffer using magnetic washer
Transfer 50 µl of each sample and Bio-Rad cell lysate control to the appropriate wells of the plate. Use detection antibody diluent as the blank.
Cover the plate with a new sheet of sealing tape and incubate in the dark for 15-18 hr at room temperature with shaking. Shake at 900-1100 rpm for 30 sec and turn down to 300-450 rpm.
Start up, warm up, and calibrate Luminex R 200 xPONET R 3.1 system
Bring all buffers and diluents to room temperature
Vortex the 20x detection antibodies for 15-20 sec at medium speed
Dilute detection antibodies to 1x with detection antibody diluent. For 96wells, mix 150 µl of 20x detection antibodies with 2850 µl of detection antibody diluent.
Slowly remove and discard the sealing tape of the assay plate
Wash the plate three times with 200 µl wash buffer using magnetic washer
Vortex the 1x detection antibodies and transfer 25 µl to each well of the assay plate
Cover the plate with a new sheet of sealing tape and incubate in the dark for 30 min at room temperature with shaking. Shake at 900-1100 rpm for 30 sec and turn down to 300-450 rpm.
Vortex the 100x Sreptavidin-PE (SA-PE) for 5 sec at medium speed
Dilute SA-PE to 1x with detection antibody diluent. For the assay in 96wells, mix 150 µl of 20x detection antibodies with 2850 µl of detection antibody diluent.
Slowly remove and discard the sealing tape of the assay plate
Wash the plate three times with 200 µl wash buffer using magnetic washer
Vortex the 1x SA-PE and transfer 50 µl to each well of the assay plate
Cover the plate with a new sheet of sealing tape and incubate in the dark for 10 min at room temperature with shaking. Shake at 900-1100 rpm for 30 sec and turn down to 300-450 rpm.
Slowly remove and discard the sealing tape of the assay plate
Wash the plate three times with 200 µl wash buffer using magnetic washer
To resuspend beads for plate reading, add 125 µl resuspension buffer to each well. Cover the plate with a new sheet of sealing tape and shake at 900-1100 rpm for 30 sec.
Slowly remove the sealing tape and place the plate on the reader
The authors have no conflicts of interest to report.

Src inhibition attenuates polyglutamine-mediated neuromuscular degeneration in spinal and bulbar muscular atrophy by Madoka Iida, Kentaro Sahashi, Naohide Kondo, Hideaki Nakatsuji, Genki Tohnai, Yutaka Tsutsumi, Seiya Noda, Ayuka Murakami, Kazunari Onodera, Yohei Okada, Masahiro Nakatochi, Yuka Tsukagoshi Okabe, Shinobu Shimizu, Masaaki Mizuno, Hiroaki Adachi, Hideyuki Okano, Gen Sobue, Masahisa Katsuno published in Nature Communications on 19 Sep, 2019 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41467-019-12282-7
